response:
Short answer: the 502s were not caused by a WAF regression or by backend HTTP 500s — they were transient Application Gateway “Bad Gateway” responses from connection/health failures between AGW and the backends during a few concentrated time windows.

What we found:
- AGW access logs for fazure-mcp showed 0 HTTP 500s from the backend over the prior 24h, but spikes of HTTP 502s around ~13:46–13:50, 14:14–14:15 and especially 14:38–14:51 UTC.
- AGW firewall logs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching the AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp rule, so the WAF was allowing the traffic (the small number of 403s were expected).
- Conclusion: the 502s were caused by gateway-level connection/timeout/health failures (App Gateway failing to successfully talk to the backend) during those windows, not by OWASP/WAF rules or application 500 errors.

AGWAccessLogs in workspace ecae2296-e4c2-47f2-9336-24599caea6be showed repeated GET /authorize requests returning 403 between 2026-03-25T14:40:58Z and 2026-03-25T14:41:34Z. AGWFirewallLogs showed custom rule BlockKnownPath blocking the full RequestUri including quer", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, a reusable WAF mitigation pattern was established for trusted internal MCP SQL traffic behind Azure Application Gateway. Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181 was receiving 403s on POST /mcp to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com because the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites allowed the VPN through the VpnOnly rule but still sent the JSON request body through OWASP managed inspection. AGWFirewallLogs showed SQL injection false positives on AR", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated fazure-mcp WAF 403s for POST /mcp from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181. Local Azure-MCP rollout artifacts showed the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites was created by copying managedRules and policySettings from the global policy, then attaching only site custom rules RateLimit, VpnOnly, BlockSensitiveFiles, BlockKnownPath, GeoBlocking, and AllowSafeFilters. The global RackspaceVPN Allow rule was not copied into the site ", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.178 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/register. Live AGW logs showed OPTIONS /register returned 200, while POST /register was blocked by global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax with rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) on client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector and redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug, followed by 949110 anomaly sco", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19 after expanding the fazure WAF lockdown, a live burst test from Rackspace VPN public IP 72.32.180.181 against https://fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com/mcp did not trigger the Application Gateway WAF RateLimit rule. Baseline responses from the VPN client were /health -> 200 and /mcp -> 401. A 130-request burst to /mcp completed with 130 HTTP 401 responses and the immediate follow-up request also returned 401. Log Analytics confirmed 132 /mcp requests with HttpStatus 401 in the 2026-", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19, live investigation of App Gateway appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax found two independent exposure paths for fazure-mcp/fazure-oauth. First, the WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax is in Prevention mode but only has Allow rules for RackspaceVPN and JanusAWS; it has no catch-all Block for non-allowed IPs. From external client IP 47.221.164.73, requests to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com/health returned 200 and /mcp returned 401 through the gateway, and AGWFire", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19 in /Users/benj8956/Downloads/Azure-MCP, created and applied dedicated per-site WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites to listeners fazure-mcp, fazure-mcp-dev, fazure-cosmos-mcp, and fazure-cosmos-mcp-dev using script scripts/fazure_per_site_waf.sh. The site policy cloned the global policy managed settings and exclusions, and applied custom rules: RateLimit priority 1 on RequestUri begins with /mcp, VpnOnly priority 2 blocking non-RackspaceVPN IPs, Blo", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19, applied and validated the fazure App Gateway lockdown for appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax using script scripts/fazure_lockdown.sh in /Users/benj8956/Downloads/Azure-MCP. WAF custom rules now show RateLimit priority 1 Block RateLimitRule, RackspaceVPN priority 2 Allow, JanusAWS priority 3 Allow, FazureMcpVpnOnly priority 7 Block, and FazureOauthVpnOnly priority 8 Block. Backend restrictions now show rg-azure-mcp-prod/azure-mcp-prod default action Deny with allow-appgw-phoenix"]}
